idea-plugin.name: GitHub Actions Tool
idea-plugin.description:
GitHub Actions Tool

A GitHub Actions workflow monitoring and execution tool for GitHub Enterprise (On-Prem) environments.
Monitor workflow status, view logs, and trigger manual dispatches — all from within the IDE.

Features

  • Workflow Monitoring — Auto-refresh workflow list and run statuses in real time
  • Manual Dispatch — Trigger workflow_dispatch workflows directly from the IDE
  • Jobs/Steps Detail — View Job tree and Step-level logs in GitHub Enterprise style
  • Log Search — Search keywords in Step logs with auto-highlight and expand
  • Status/Branch Filters — Filter runs by conclusion (success, failure, etc.) and branch
  • Quick Search — Instant name search in the Run list (Speed Search)
  • VCS Auto-Detection — Automatically detect GitHub server/owner/repository from Git remote
  • IntelliJ GitHub Account Integration — Use tokens from registered IntelliJ GitHub accounts (no separate PAT required)

Layout

The Tool Window consists of 4 panels:

  1. Workflow Tree — Full workflow list. Select "All History" or a specific workflow
  2. Run List — Recent runs for the selected workflow. Status/branch filters available
  3. Jobs Detail Tree — Job list for the selected Run, displayed as a grouped tree
  4. Step Log Panel — Collapsible log view per Step for the selected Job

Authentication

Two authentication methods are supported:

  • IntelliJ GitHub Account (default, recommended) — Automatically uses tokens from Settings > Version Control > GitHub
  • Personal Access Token — Uncheck the account option in Settings > Tools > GitHub Actions Tool, then enter your token

Usage

  • Click the GitHub Actions tab in the bottom bar (or View > Tool Windows > GitHub Actions)
  • Select a workflow → click a Run → click a Job → view Step logs
  • Use the Dispatch button or right-click a workflow to trigger manually
  • Double-click a Run to open its GitHub web page in the browser

Requirements

  • IntelliJ IDEA 2024.1 or later
  • Git and GitHub plugins enabled (bundled by default)
  • Git remote configured in the project
